channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Interval Certifications for Multilayered Perceptrons via Lattice Traversal
item.description: arXiv:2607.08773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this work we present a rigorous theoretical framework to a foundational problem of AI safety, namely adversarial robustness. In particular, we show that the adversarial robustness problem can be reduced to a lattice traversal problem. Each element...

item.title: CogniConsole: Externalizing Inference-Time Control as a Formal Abstraction for Reliable LLM Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2607.08774v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliability in large language model (LLM) systems is typically framed as a function of model capability. We challenge this by demonstrating that reliability is significantly influenced by \emph{inference-time control} -- the computational layer...

item.title: GATS: Graph-Augmented Tree Search with Layered World Models for Efficient Agent Planning
item.description: arXiv:2607.08894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have shown promise in multi-step planning tasks, but existing approaches like LATS (Language Agent Tree Search) and ReAct rely heavily on LLM inference during planning, leading to high computational costs and...

item.title: Long-Horizon-Terminal-Bench: Testing the Limits of Agents on Long-Horizon Terminal Tasks with Dense Reward-Based Grading
item.description: arXiv:2607.08964v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents have become capable of autonomously completing short, well-specified tasks. However, existing terminal benchmarks largely focus on simple problems that finish within minutes and are evaluated only by their final outcome. This setup overlooks...

item.title: A Formalization of the Mean-Field Derivation of the Vlasov Equation: AI-Assisted Lean Formalization as a Strategy Game
item.description: arXiv:2607.08986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We formalize a research result in the Lean 4 proof assistant by having a mathematician direct an AI system, and frame the activity as a formalization game. The objective is to turn a LaTeX document into Lean. The game is won when the development...

item.title: A Unified Approach to Interpreting Knowledge Distillation for Large Language Models via Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2607.08776v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the success of knowledge distillation (KD) in Large Language Models (LLMs), the underlying mechanism behind its efficacy remains unclear. In this paper, we propose a unified approach to explore the common mechanism of various KD methods using...

item.title: iLENS: Interpretable LLM-Guided Mixture-of-Experts for Neuroimaging Survival Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2607.08778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a complex neurodegenerative disorder that continues to impact millions of people worldwide. Predicting AD conversion during the prodromal stage remains critical for disease understanding and patient care. As such, survival...

item.title: Signed Symmetric Quantization for Few-Bit Integers
item.description: arXiv:2607.08779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The signed integer alphabet contains one more negative representable value than positive. Yet, by convention, the standard symmetric integer quantizer fixes its scale to be strictly positive, which assigns this extra representable value to the...

item.title: Sticky Routing: Training MoE Models for Memory-Efficient Inference
item.description: arXiv:2607.08780v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models activate only a sparse subset of experts per token, yet consecutive tokens frequently activate different experts -- causing constant weight swapping between slow storage and fast memory on edge devices. Existing...

item.title: Reward Transport: Property Control in Flow Matching via Noise-Space Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2607.08781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The coupling in flow matching -- the rule pairing noise vectors with data points -- is typically treated as a computational choice. We show that this coupling can instead serve as an alignment interface: by matching noise and data according to a...

item.title: HALO: Hybrid Adaptive Latent Reasoning for Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.08775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study how to improve a frozen pretrained language model with a small amount of adaptive extra computation. A simple approach is to add additional refinement steps on top of the backbone hidden states, but fixed extra refinement can be wasteful: a...

item.title: An Emergent Mirage: Is Emergent Misalignment and Realignment Indeed a Robust Phenomenon?
item.description: arXiv:2607.09053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has reported Emergent Misalignment (EM), where language models fine-tuned on narrow, domain-specific misaligned datasets abruptly acquire broadly misaligned behavior, alongside evidence that this behavior can be reversed through limited...

item.title: AgentKGV: Agentic LLM-RAG Framework with Two-Stage Training for the Fact Verification of Knowledge Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2607.09092v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are often automatically constructed from large-scale corpora, but they inevitably contain factual errors due to noisy sources and extraction failures, and verifying them reliably at industrial scale remains a critical challenge....

item.title: PRecG: Legal Precedent Retrieval with Graph Neural Networks and Rhetorical Role Segmentation
item.description: arXiv:2607.09094v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Legal precedent retrieval is a fundamental task in legal case preparation, planning, litigation strategy, and legal research. Current approaches for automatic precedent retrieval map legal documents to a low-dimensional semantic space and compute...

item.title: Augmenting Fundamental Analysis with Large Language Models: A RAG-Based System for Generating Investor Briefs
item.description: arXiv:2607.09121v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this study, we examine the opportunities brought by Large Language Models (LLMs) to various aspects of fundamental analysis of companies based on their reports as well as data and documents describing macroeconomic situation like GDP and inflation...

item.title: Major Breakthrough: CZ-10B Threads the Needle for Net Recovery, But What Remains for Private Rockets?
item.description: As CASC Long March 10B proves state-owned reusable rocket capability, private rocket companies face existential questions about their role versus delivering incremental innovation.

item.title: Paper-Thin Smart Material Adhesion Increased 400x: NPU Discovers Electric-Activated Grip Holding 10-Pound Dumbbells
item.description: Northwestern Polytechnical University develops smart adhesive material that activates 400x stronger grip when electrified, opening applications in chip manufacturing, soft robotics, and precision assembly.

item.title: After CZ-10B, the Next Exam for China Private Commercial Rocket Companies
item.description: With CASC Long March 10B now demonstrating reusable rocket capability, China private rocket startups face a higher competitive bar requiring larger payloads and operational reusability.

item.title: Tencent WorkBuddy Beginner Guide: A Local AI Agent Tailored for Chinese Users That Actually Does Your Work
item.description: Tencent launches WorkBuddy, a local AI coding agent built on CodeBuddy with Hunyuan Hy3 model, integrating WeChat for file management, automation, and task execution.

item.title: A New Era Begins: Sugon Dawn 8000 Marks China Entry Into 100,000-Card AI Computing Infrastructure
item.description: Sugon Dawn 8000 Dengfeng becomes China first fully domestic 100K-card super-AI fusion cluster, built on Hygon processors and connecting to the National Supercomputing Internet.

item.title: Cloudflare dash is unavailable
item.description: Jul 12, 22:30 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 12, 22:23 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 12, 22:05 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 12, 21:40 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: 6 months to live for open models
item.description: The most serious test to date of open source AI’s viability is happening right now.

item.title: Elevated conversation error rates on ChatGPT for iOS
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating an issue where some users may encounter 403 errors when loading or using ChatGPT conversations on iOS. We will provide updates as we learn more.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard login issue
item.description: Jul 12, 09:43 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue where some users are unable to login to the Cloudflare Dashboard. We are working to mitigate the problem and will update shortly.

item.title: INC20000085
item.description: Jul 11, 19:36 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've implemented the fix for this issue and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: Compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm Release Drops Rust Infostealer During Install
item.description: The jscrambler npm package was compromised, and simply installing its 8.14.0 release runs an infostealer on your machine. Published on July 11, 2026, the malicious version carries a preinstall hook that drops and executes a native binary, one build each for Windows, macOS, and...

item.title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of sustained cyber espionage activity against several Pakistani law enforcement organizations undertaken by suspected China- and India-aligned threat actors between February 2024 and April 2026. "At Balochistan Police, the compromised assets included...

item.title: Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign
item.description: Multiple campaigns are using ghost accounts to map GitHub organizations, including their repositories and members. The post Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Pages Dashboard issues
item.description: Jul 11, 10:50 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 11, 10:43 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 11, 10:34 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 11, 10:22 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Elevated Errors for Sora API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Sora (Operational)

item.title: Critical Zimbra Flaw Could Let Crafted Emails Run Malicious Code in User Sessions
item.description: Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability has been described as a case of stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that could allow specially crafted emails to...

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day after a week of nonstop model releases

item.title: OpenAI website and Help Center content may be unavailable
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external security threat." The company has temporarily disabled access to the affected accounts, a step it says...

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases. The compromised version, @injectivelabs/sdk-ts@1.20.21, came embedded with...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 10, 15:02 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 10, 14:36 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Abnormal AI sued by Anthropic, AssuranceAmerica data breach affects 7 million people, NSA brings back TAO. The post In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops appeared first on...

item.title: INC20000082
item.description: Jul 10, 14:30 UTC Investigating - Current status: We're investigating an issue with Snowflake Data Cloud. We'll provide an update within 1 hour.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified regions may be unable to connect to Snowflake via private connectivity solutions, such as...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5
item.description: Jul 10, 13:00 UTC Resolved - Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Sonnet 4.5 between 6:00am PT / 1:00pm UTC and 8:15am PT / 3:15pm UTC.

item.title: Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description: Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, was sentenced to 70 months for helping the BlackCat/Alphv group. The post Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on...

item.title: China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force
item.description: Both foes and allies have targeted the Balochistan Police force in Pakistan for at least two years, according to SentinelOne. The post China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers
item.description: The attackers call victims to direct them to phishing websites mirroring Microsoft Entra ID login pages. The post Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
item.description: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might...

item.title: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT 5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, Codex becomes ChatGPT superapp
item.description: A big day for OpenAI.

item.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 3): Attention is all you profile
Users are experiencing elevated errors when selecting models
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the...

item.title: Wat3R: Underwater 3D Geometry Learning without Annotations
item.description: Estimating 3D geometry in underwater environments presents unique challenges due to light attenuation, scattering, and the absence of large-scale, high-quality 3D annotations. Pioneering methods rely on massive dense annotations that are impractical in underwater settings. In this paper, we propose...

item.title: Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning for Panoramic Outdoor Reconstruction
item.description: Scaling 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) to large outdoor scenes is costly in both data acquisition and computation. Adopting panoramic images with equirectangular projection (ERP) can reduce capture effort via their full $360^{\circ}$ field of view, yet the resulting omnipresent visibility invalidates...
